patient.query: When I think about skipping work, it's like this battle in my head. Part of me knows I should go, that I have responsibilities and people counting on me. But then, there’s this other voice telling me it’s just not worth it—that it’ll be overwhelming and I won’t be able to handle it. I feel so trapped between wanting to do the right thing and just wanting to hide away. It’s frustrating.

If you showed any weakness, it felt like you were letting everyone down. So, I guess I internalized that. It’s hard to shake off that feeling like I have to live up to those expectations, even if it feels impossible some days.
